title: nvm_read unconditionally pays metadata round-trip
description: nvm_read always calls nvm_metadata to discover object length before reading, even when the object length is already known to the caller. Each metadata call is a full client-server round-trip over the transport (TCP/UDS/SHM). At scale — tight loops reading many small NVM objects — this doubles the IPC cost. nvm_read_raw exists as the escape hatch but it is the secondary API; callers who reach for the obvious nvm_read pay the cost without realising it. Expected impact: 2x round-trips per read for any code path that already knows the length (e.g., fixed-size counters, certificates of known structure).
